skill.name: releasing-versions
skill.description: Guides release preparation with validation, version updates, documentation checks, build verification, and controlled git tagging.
skill.summary: Prepare software releases with structured validation, version checks, documentation review, and release commands.
skill.author: BPSAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified many external command examples, but they are release workflow commands rather than hidden malware. The weak cryptography alert on SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive, and hidden file access is limited to documented project files. Publish with a warning because the skill may lead agents to run build, install, git, and package upload commands.

medium_findings.title: Release Workflow Runs External Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run test, security scan, documentation, build, install, and git commands. These commands are legitimate for release management, but they can modify local state or execute project tooling, so users should review before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and semantically aligned with release preparation. The risk is operational rather than malicious because commands are visible and mostly standard release checks.

medium_findings.title: Publishing Commands Require Human Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The report template includes commands for pushing git refs and uploading distribution files. The skill tells the agent not to push until the user reviews, but publication commands still represent a sensitive release action.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive commands are directly shown, and the skill includes a review gate before pushing. The issue is controlled but important for marketplace users to understand.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but that line is only a natural language description mentioning security checks. No weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains frontmatter text only. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or another weak cryptographic algorithm in that location.

low_findings.title: Hidden Project File Access Is Limited
low_findings.description: The skill references a .paircoder configuration path when comparing template files. This is expected for the project workflow and does not show broad hidden directory scanning or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path is a specific documented project configuration file. No network transfer or secret collection behavior is present near the reference.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing Shell Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands such as package installation, build cleanup, git staging, git tagging, and package upload. These should remain visible to users and should not run without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in bash blocks and have clear state-changing effects. The surrounding instructions indicate release intent, not concealment.

content.value_statement: Release preparation often misses tests, documentation, or version updates. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured release checklist with validation steps and review gates.
actual_capabilities: Checks sprint task status before release preparation.
Runs or requests test, coverage, secret scan, and dependency scan steps.
Guides version updates across package, init, capability, and configuration files.
Verifies release documentation such as changelogs, readme files, and feature matrices.
Builds and verifies a Python package before git commit and tag preparation.
Produces a release readiness summary with blockers, warnings, and next commands.
limitations: Assumes a BPSAI PairCoder project layout and command set.
Does not publish automatically and requires user confirmation for push commands.
Cannot determine whether stale documentation is acceptable without project context.
May need adjustment for projects that do not use Python packaging or cookiecutter templates.

use_cases.title: Prepare a CLI Package Release
use_cases.description: Run validation, update version references, build the package, and prepare a git tag.

use_cases.title: Review Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Check tasks, tests, coverage, security scans, and documentation before approving a release.

use_cases.title: Keep Project Templates Synced
use_cases.description: Compare live project configuration with cookiecutter template files before shipping changes.

prompt_templates.title: Start Release Checks
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a basic release readiness review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the releasing-versions workflow to check whether this project is ready for a release. Report blockers before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Version Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Update version references for a target release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare release version X.Y.Z. Find the version files, list required changes, and ask before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm that release notes and docs match the release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the release documentation for version X.Y.Z. Check the changelog, readme, and feature matrix, then summarize stale or missing items.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Release Candidate
prompt_templates.scenario: Run the full workflow through build and tag preparation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full releasing-versions workflow for X.Y.Z. Stop before pushing or publishing, and provide a release readiness report.

output_examples.output: The release is blocked because coverage is below the required threshold and the changelog has no 2.9.0 entry. Version files were not changed.

output_examples.input: Check whether the documentation is current for this release.
output_examples.output: The changelog and readme are current. The feature matrix was last updated outside the freshness window and should be reviewed before release.

output_examples.input: Finish release preparation but do not publish.
output_examples.output: The package built and installed successfully. A commit and annotated tag are prepared for review, but push and upload commands were not run.

best_practices: Run validation commands from a clean working tree whenever possible.
Review every generated version, changelog, commit, and tag change before publishing.
Keep the release summary focused on blockers, warnings, and user-approved next steps.
anti_patterns: Do not push tags or upload packages without explicit user approval.
Do not continue a release when tests, coverage, or secret scans fail.
Do not assume the cookiecutter template must match every project-specific value.

faq.question: Does this skill publish releases automatically?
faq.answer: No. It prepares release steps and explicitly stops before push or upload commands until the user confirms.

faq.question: Which projects does it fit best?
faq.answer: It fits BPSAI PairCoder style Python projects with pytest, changelog files, cookiecutter templates, and git tagging.

faq.question: Can it update version files?
faq.answer: Yes. It identifies expected version locations and can guide or perform updates after user review.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses visible shell commands that can change files, install packages, create tags, or prepare publishing steps.

faq.question: Does it scan for secrets?
faq.answer: It includes a release step for project secret scanning through the expected project command.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is written as agent guidance and supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code usage.
